Homepage.title: Free Online Tools
Homepage.subtitle: Powerful utilities for developers, designers, and professionals. Create UUIDs, encode URLs, generate passwords, process images, and more — all free, fast, and privacy-focused.
Homepage.developmentDesc: Code editors, frameworks, and development tools
Homepage.recentPostsDesc: Stay updated with our latest insights, tutorials, and tips about online tools and web development.
Homepage.privacyDescription: All tools run locally in your browser. No data is sent to servers, ensuring your privacy and security.
Homepage.seoDescription: Discover powerful free online tools including password generators, Base64 encoders, UUID generators, and more. Boost your productivity.
Homepage.seoKeywords: online tools, free tools, password generator, Base64, UUID, developer tools

errors.emptyFields: Please enter both height and weight
errors.positiveNumbers: Height and weight must be positive numbers

BMRCalculator.title: BMR Calculator
BMRCalculator.description: Calculate your Basal Metabolic Rate (BMR) and daily calorie needs.

activityLevels.veryActive: Extra Active (very hard exercise & physical job)

BMRCalculator.bmrExplanation: Basal Metabolic Rate (BMR) is the number of calories your body needs to maintain basic physiological functions while at complete rest.
BMRCalculator.tdeeExplanation: Total Daily Energy Expenditure (TDEE) is the total number of calories you burn in a day, including your BMR plus additional calories burned through physical activity and digestion.
BMRCalculator.bmrDetailedExplanation: Your BMR represents your minimum calorie needs, while your TDEE represents your maintenance calories. For weight loss, consume fewer calories than your TDEE; for weight gain, consume more. These are estimates and may vary based on individual factors.

errors.positiveNumbers: Age, height, and weight must be positive numbers
errors.ageRange: Age must be between 15 and 80 years

CarbonFootprintCalculator.title: Carbon Footprint Calculator
CarbonFootprintCalculator.description: Calculate your carbon footprint based on your lifestyle and consumption habits.
CarbonFootprintCalculator.consumptionLevelDesc: 0% = Minimal consumption (mostly essentials), 50% = Average consumer, 100% = Frequent shopper (luxury goods, latest gadgets)
CarbonFootprintCalculator.recyclingLevelDesc: 0% = No recycling, 50% = Recycle some materials, 100% = Comprehensive recycling of all possible materials
CarbonFootprintCalculator.footprintExplanation: Your carbon footprint is the total amount of greenhouse gases (primarily CO₂) released into the atmosphere as a result of your activities.
CarbonFootprintCalculator.footprintDetailedExplanation: The global average carbon footprint is about 4 tons per person per year. In developed countries, the average is typically 10-20 tons. To limit global warming to 1.5°C, the global average needs to drop to about 2 tons per person by 2050.
CarbonFootprintCalculator.reductionTips: How to Reduce Your Carbon Footprint

tips.transportation: Transportation: Use public transit, walk or bike when possible, consider an electric vehicle, or reduce air travel.
tips.diet: Diet: Reduce meat consumption (especially beef), minimize food waste, and choose local and seasonal foods.
tips.energy: Energy: Improve home insulation, use energy-efficient appliances, switch to renewable energy sources, and be mindful of heating/cooling usage.
tips.consumption: Consumption: Buy less, choose durable products, repair items instead of replacing them, and recycle properly.

errors.positiveNumbers: All values must be positive numbers
